title: Creating read-only column values
excerpt:
I don’t see it documented in the API, but it is definitely possible… 
how do you create a read-only column? i see in the Todoist integration, they create a column that’s read-only. does anybody know how to do this?

posts.author: cryptocharlie404 (charlie)
posts.cookedHtml:
I don’t see it documented in the API, but it is definitely possible…

how do you create a read-only column? i see in the Todoist integration, they create a column that’s read-only. does anybody know how to do this?

posts.author: dsilva (Daniel Silva)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @cryptocharlie404 - apologies for the delay here.

Let me check in with the team on this one - at a glance I don’t see a way of doing this through the API, which makes me wonder if perhaps the integrations are leveraging the SDK for this.

-Daniel
